# Discussion

## Interpretation of Results

<!--
- Which models performed best and why?
- Which features emerged as most predictive?
- Do results align with clinical expectations?
-->

## Hypothesis Evaluation

### H1: Distinguishable Profiles at Baseline

<!--
Verified / Partially verified / Falsified?
What evidence supports or contradicts the hypothesis?
-->

### H2: Memory Features as Primary Discriminators

<!--
Were memory tests (LOGIMEM, MEMUNITS, etc.) indeed the strongest predictors?
Or did executive function or behavioral features contribute more?
-->

### H3: Added Value of APOE ε4

<!--
Did genetic information improve model performance?
Discuss the biomarker subanalysis results.
-->

### H4: Characteristics of Disease Regression

<!--
What distinguished MCI reverters from stable MCI?
Are the findings clinically plausible?
-->

## Comparison with Existing Literature

<!--
How do your findings compare to published studies?
- Agreement or disagreement with known risk factors
- Novel findings or unexpected results
-->

## Limitations

<!--
Be honest and thorough:
- Retrospective observational design
- Potential selection bias in NACC data
- Small biomarker sample
- Missing data and imputation assumptions
- No causal claims possible
- Practice effects in longitudinal cognitive testing
-->

## Future Directions

<!--
- Incorporating neuroimaging data
- Survival analysis for time-to-conversion
- Deep learning on larger, multimodal datasets
- External validation on non-NACC cohorts
-->
